php.name: PHP
php.summary: Primary backend language used for building and maintaining high-load production systems.
highlights: Extensive commercial backend development across multiple production projects
Led and executed migrations across major versions (5.6 → 8.x)
Strong use of OOP and modern PHP features (strict types, 7+ ecosystem)
Deep debugging experience, including vendor-level investigation
Delivered wide range of features and fixes across long-lived codebases

symfony.name: Symfony
symfony.summary: Main framework for structured backend development, integrations, and scalable application design.
highlights: Worked across Symfony 3.2 → 6.4, including multiple major migrations
Deep understanding of service container (DI, tagging, _instanceof, advanced config)
Strong experience with complex forms (multistep, nested, custom components)
Used Messenger + RabbitMQ for async processing
Integrated external systems (REST APIs, SOAP, payment providers)
Built and maintained reusable Composer packages for internal platforms
Led code reviews and mentored developers in Symfony ecosystem

doctrine.name: Doctrine ORM
doctrine.summary: ORM used for data mapping and database interaction in Symfony applications.
highlights: Worked with entity relationships, inheritance, and complex mappings
Handled performance issues such as N+1 queries and inefficient hydration
Optimized queries and data access patterns in legacy systems
Used Doctrine in long-lived applications with evolving schemas

mysql.name: MySQL
mysql.summary: Primary relational database used in backend systems with focus on query performance and data reliability.
highlights: Long-term MySQL usage in production applications
Optimized complex queries in legacy and high-load systems
Worked with joins, indexing strategies, and query performance tuning
Used temporary tables and stored procedures for complex data processing and performance optimization
Handled schema evolution in long-lived projects
Investigated and resolved production issues related to data consistency and performance

redis.name: Redis
redis.summary: In-memory data store used to improve performance and manage transient application state.
highlights: Used caching to reduce database load and improve response times
Worked with session storage and short-lived application data
Applied Redis for fast-access data in backend-heavy systems
Supported performance optimization without major architectural changes

rabbitmq.name: RabbitMQ
rabbitmq.summary: Message broker used for asynchronous processing and decoupling backend workflows.
highlights: Used RabbitMQ with Symfony Messenger for async task processing
Designed queue-based workflows to decouple services and improve reliability
Worked with retries, delayed processing, and failure handling
Applied patterns such as Inbox to ensure consistent message processing
Managed worker processes using Supervisor for stable background execution

java.name: Java
java.summary: Secondary backend language explored to expand ecosystem and architectural perspective.
highlights: Completed structured training focused on enterprise Java development practices
Built educational backend projects using Java and Spring ecosystem
Used Java for small tools and exploratory implementations
Worked with core collections and streams API
Compared Java-based service design with PHP/Symfony approaches

spring-boot.name: Spring Boot
spring-boot.summary: Framework used to explore convention-driven Java service development.
highlights: Built backend services using Spring Boot in educational projects
Learned dependency injection, configuration, and service structuring patterns
Explored typical enterprise Java project setup and conventions
Used it to understand differences from Symfony-based architecture

data-structures.name: Data Structures and Algorithms
data-structures.summary: Applied as part of backend problem-solving and implementation work.
highlights: Applied data structures and algorithmic thinking in backend implementations
Used appropriate structures (arrays, maps, queues, heaps) when designing data flows and processing logic
Continuously practiced problem-solving with various structures, including graphs and queues, using Java

llm-integration.name: LLM Integration
llm-integration.summary: Integration of large language models into backend systems and development workflows.
highlights: Integrated LLM APIs into backend applications for real use cases
Used LLMs to support development workflows and tasks implementation
Worked with self-hosted LLM deployments for controlled environments
Handled request/response flows, limits, and practical integration constraints

rag.name: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation)
rag.summary: Approach for combining LLMs with external data sources to improve response accuracy.
highlights: Designed and implemented RAG workflows for backend applications
Implemented document ingestion and chunking strategies for retrieval
Worked with embeddings and vector-based search for relevant context selection
Built test cases to evaluate RAG response quality and retrieval accuracy

n8n.name: n8n
n8n.summary: Workflow automation tool used to orchestrate backend integrations and AI-driven processes.
highlights: Integrated backend applications with external services using n8n workflows
Used n8n to orchestrate AI-driven processes and data pipelines
Reduced manual integration effort through workflow-based automation

linux.name: Linux
linux.summary: Primary environment for backend development, automation, and system-level tasks.
highlights: Worked in Linux-based environments for backend development and operations
Created Bash scripts to automate development and deployment processes
Configured servers and handled environment setup
Applied basic security configuration for production environments
Handled system-level tasks and troubleshooting

docker.name: Docker
docker.summary: Containerization tool used to build and manage full application environments.
highlights: Created production-ready Docker Compose configurations
Designed multi-service environments with isolated components
Configured services such as Node, RabbitMQ workers, cron, and monitoring tools
Used environment-based configuration via .env for flexible setups
Containerized full application stack for consistent development and deployment

git.name: Git
git.summary: Version control system used for structured team collaboration and controlled delivery workflows.
highlights: Introduced and maintained Git-based development workflows (Git Flow)
Worked with branching strategies for feature development and releases
Supported team collaboration through structured merge and review processes

ci-cd.name: CI/CD
ci-cd.summary: Continuous integration and deployment workflows used to automate testing and delivery processes.
highlights: Implemented CI pipelines to run tests and static code analysis
Automated merge request validation workflows
Implemented CD processes to deploy applications to environments
Improved delivery consistency and reduced manual deployment effort

new-relic.name: New Relic
new-relic.summary: Monitoring platform used to track application performance and system behavior in production.
highlights: Implemented full application monitoring across backend, frontend, and infrastructure
Tracked performance metrics, network behavior, and server resource usage
Monitored frontend performance and JavaScript errors across user sessions
Used monitoring data to investigate and diagnose production issues
Optimized data collection to control resource usage and costs

javascript.name: JavaScript
javascript.summary: Core language for building interactive frontend functionality and integrating UI with backend systems.
highlights: 9+ years working with JavaScript across multiple production projects
Implemented dynamic UI behavior and client-side logic
Worked across frontend and backend boundaries in full-stack scenarios
Used JavaScript to support tooling and integration tasks

vue.name: Vue.js
vue.summary: Framework used for building structured and maintainable frontend interfaces.
highlights: Built component-based user interfaces using Vue.js
Worked with reactive state and modular frontend architecture
Integrated frontend components with backend APIs
Worked with Vuex state management
Used Vue in production applications with real user flows

html.name: HTML
html.summary: Markup used to structure frontend interfaces and integrate with backend-rendered views.
highlights: Built structured and semantic layouts for web applications
Worked with template-based rendering in backend-driven systems
Ensured clear structure and maintainability of UI markup

css.name: CSS
css.summary: Styling used to build responsive and usable interfaces for web applications.
highlights: Implemented responsive layouts and UI styling for production systems
Improved usability and visual clarity of application interfaces
Worked with modular and maintainable styling approaches

scss.name: SCSS
scss.summary: Preprocessor used to structure and maintain larger stylesheets.
highlights: Used variables, nesting, and partials to organize styles
Improved maintainability of complex styling structures
Applied SCSS in real-world frontend workflows

webpack.name: Webpack
webpack.summary: Build tool used to manage and bundle frontend assets in production applications.
highlights: Configured Webpack for frontend asset bundling in Symfony projects
Worked with build pipelines for JavaScript and SCSS
Configured Babel to support different JavaScript environments
Handled asset optimization and environment-specific builds
Used Webpack as part of full-stack development workflows
